REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SAED NIJEM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, DECLARATORY RELIEF, QUIET TITLE AND ELDER ABUSE IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY HAS SHIFTED ITS BURDEN. OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY OR CREATE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING MOVING DEFENDANT'S LIABILITY FOR THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF WARRANTY OF QUIET ENJOYMENT AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXISTS REGARDING PLAINTIFFS' STATUS AS TENANTS; BREACHES OF WARRANTY OF QUIET ENJOYMENT, AND PLAINTIFFS' EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAED NIJEM's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication for causes of action for breach of contract, declaratory relief, quiet title and elder abuse is GRANTED. Moving party has shifted its burden. Opposing party failed to cite any authority or create triable issues of fact regarding moving defendant's liability for these causes of action. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to causes of action for breach of warranty of quiet enjoyment and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Triable issues of fact exists regarding Plaintiffs' status as tenants; breaches of warranty of quiet enjoyment, and Plaintiffs' emotional distress. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Albert Boasber, (650)755-6960 for Plaintiff/Opposing party Paranee Ratreesawad.
Nelson Goodell, (415)495-3950 for Defendant/Moving party Randolph Caruso.

Argued. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT RANDOLPH CARUSO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to ament to attach exhibits and to verify the complaint. Demurrer is otherwise overruled- Defendant is improperly raising issues of fact on demurrer. The Court notes that plaintiff's counsel failed to comply with SFLR 2.6B and Court's December 15, 2015 minute order. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
